cards.title: Hypertension — Naturopathic Complete Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
BP Targets
Normal: <120/80 · Elevated: 120–129/<80 · Stage 1 HTN: 130–139/80–89 · Stage 2 HTN: ≥140/90 · Hypertensive crisis: >180/120 with organ damage → EMERGENCY.
InterventionExpected ↓ SBPEvidenceProtocol
DASH diet8–14 mmHg★★★★High fruit/veg/low-fat dairy, low sodium <2.3g/day
Sodium restriction2–8 mmHg★★★★Target <1.5–2.3g/day; read labels; avoid processed food
Exercise4–9 mmHg★★★★150 min moderate aerobic/week; resistance 2x/week
Weight loss1mmHg per kg★★★★Target BMI <25; even 5kg significantly reduces BP
Potassium ↑3–5 mmHg★★★3500–4700mg/day from food: banana, avocado, spinach, sweet potato
Magnesium3–4 mmHg★★★Mg glycinate 400mg/day — especially in deficiency
Garlic (aged extract)5–10 mmHg SBP★★★Kyolic aged garlic 600–1200mg/day
Hibiscus tea6–7 mmHg SBP★★★3 cups/day — ACE inhibitor-like mechanism
HydrotherapyVariable★★Warm foot baths, cold packs to neck, contrast showers
Alcohol reduction2–4 mmHg★★★<14 units/week men, <7 units/week women
🟡 Pearl — Refer When
Refer to physician immediately: BP >180/120 · Secondary HTN features (young patient, resistant HTN, haematuria, acute kidney injury) · End-organ damage (headache + papilloedema, chest pain, acute neurological symptoms) · BP not controlled after 3 months of aggressive lifestyle modification. Naturopathic intervention is effective for Stage 1 and as adjunct for Stage 2 — never withhold medication when clinically indicated.

cards.title: Dyslipidaemia — Naturopathic Management
cards._legacyHtml:
InterventionLDL EffectHDL EffectTG EffectDose
Plant sterols/stanols↓ 10–15%NeutralNeutral2g/day with meals
Soluble fibre (psyllium, oats, pectin)↓ 5–10%NeutralNeutral10–15g/day
Fish oil (EPA+DHA)May ↑ LDL slightly↑ slightly↓ 25–30%3–4g EPA+DHA/day for TG
Red yeast rice↓ 15–25%Neutral↓ slightly1200–2400mg/day — contains monacolin K (lovastatin)
Berberine↓ 20–25%↑ slightly↓ 25%500mg TDS with meals
Niacin (B3)↓ 10%↑ 20–35% ★↓ 20–50%1–3g/day — flushing limits use
Garlic (aged)↓ 5–10%Neutral↓ slightly600–1200mg/day aged garlic
Artichoke leaf extract↓ 10%↑ slightly↓ slightly1800mg/day standardised
🟡 Red Yeast Rice Warning
Red yeast rice contains monacolin K — chemically identical to lovastatin (a prescription statin). It WORKS — but carries the same risks: myopathy, rhabdomyolysis, liver toxicity, CoQ10 depletion. Always supplement CoQ10 100–200mg/day alongside. Contraindicated in pregnancy, liver disease, myopathy history. Monitor CK and LFTs. Not for use in patients already on statins (double dosing). Disclose this to patients who believe they are "avoiding medication" with RYR.

cards.title: Heart Failure — Naturopathic Adjunct Care
cards._legacyHtml:
Important
Heart failure is a medical emergency requiring physician management. Naturopathic interventions are ADJUNCTS to medical treatment — never replace diuretics, ACEi, beta-blockers, or device therapy.
InterventionBenefitDoseEvidence
CoQ10Improves ejection fraction, reduces mortality (Q-SYMBIO trial)300mg/day in divided doses★★★★ Strong
MagnesiumReduces arrhythmia risk, improves exercise toleranceMg glycinate 400mg/day★★★ Good
Omega-3 (fish oil)GISSI-HF trial: reduced mortality 9%1g EPA+DHA/day★★★ Good
Hawthorn (Crataegus)Positive inotrope, vasodilator, antioxidant900mg extract/day (NYHA I–II)★★★ Good
Sodium restrictionReduces fluid retention, rehospitalisation<2g/day★★★★ Strong
Fluid restrictionPrevents fluid overload1.5–2L/day (severe HF)★★★ Good — physician guided

cards.title: Atherosclerosis Prevention — Naturopathic Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
Atherosclerosis is Reversible
Ornish trial: intensive lifestyle programme reversed coronary artery disease measurably at 1 year. Naturopathic CVD prevention has strong primary and secondary prevention evidence.
TargetInterventionMechanismEvidence
LDL reductionSoluble fibre 10g + Plant sterols 2g + Berberine 500mg TDSBile acid sequestration + cholesterol absorption down + PCSK9 downStrong
Inflammation (hsCRP)Fish oil 3g EPA+DHA + Curcumin 1g + Mediterranean dietCOX inhibition, NF-kB down, anti-inflammatoryStrong
Endothelial functionL-arginine 3–6g/day + Beetroot juice 250ml + ExerciseNO synthesis up, vasodilationGood
Oxidative stressCoQ10 200mg + Vit C 1g + Resveratrol 250mgMitochondrial antioxidant, LDL oxidation downGood
Platelet aggregationFish oil 3g + Garlic 600mg + Nattokinase 2000FUThromboxane A2 down, fibrinolysis upGood
🟡 Coronary Calcium Score (CAC)
The Coronary Artery Calcium Score (CT scan) is the single best predictor of future cardiovascular events — better than cholesterol, CRP, or Framingham score alone. CAC = 0: 15-year event rate <1% — lifestyle only. CAC 1–100: moderate risk — aggressive lifestyle intervention. CAC >100: high risk — statin therapy strongly indicated alongside naturopathic intervention. Order CAC for intermediate Framingham risk patients and those questioning whether to start statins.

cards.title: Palpitations — Assessment & Naturopathic Management
cards._legacyHtml:
Rule Out Serious Causes First ★
Palpitations with syncope, chest pain, dyspnoea, or family history of sudden death → ECG + Holter + cardiology referral urgently. Never manage palpitations naturopathically without excluding structural cardiac disease.
TypeECG FindingNaturopathic RoleRefer?
Sinus tachycardiaRate >100, normal P-QRSAddress cause: anxiety, anaemia, thyroid, caffeine, dehydrationIf no obvious cause
PVCs / PACs (ectopics)Early beats, compensatory pauseMg 400mg/day, potassium optimisation, reduce caffeine and alcoholIf >10,000 PVCs/24hr or symptoms
SVT (paroxysmal)Narrow complex 150–250bpmVagal manoeuvres (Valsalva), breathing techniques, MgYes — EP study consideration
Atrial fibrillationIrregularly irregular, no P wavesTrigger management: alcohol, caffeine, sleep apnoea, electrolytesYes — anticoagulation decision
Functional palpitationsNormal ECG and HolterAnxiety management, HRV biofeedback, Mg, cut caffeineAfter cardiac excluded
🟡 Magnesium and Arrhythmia
Magnesium is nature's antiarrhythmic. Mg2+ stabilises cardiac cell membranes and is essential for Na/K-ATPase function. Deficiency increases automaticity, ectopy, and arrhythmia risk. IV magnesium is standard of care for Torsades de Pointes. Oral Mg glycinate 400mg/day reduces PVC burden and SVT frequency. Check RBC magnesium — normal serum Mg does not exclude intracellular deficiency, which is common.

cards.title: Coronary Artery Disease — Naturopathic Adjunct
cards._legacyHtml:
CAD Naturopathic Protocol
BNYS manages risk factors naturopathically and supports post-MI rehabilitation with evidence-based supplements. Never delay urgent cardiac care.
BiomarkerStandardOptimal Target
LDL CholesterolStandard test<1.8 mmol/L high risk; <1.4 very high risk
Lp(a)Often omitted — request specifically<30 mg/dL. Genetic — minimally diet-responsive
ApoBBetter predictor in insulin resistance<0.8 g/L (low); <0.65 (very high risk)
hs-CRPJUPITER trial — predicts events independent of LDL<1.0 mg/L optimal. >3.0 = high inflammatory CV risk
Omega-3 IndexEPA+DHA % of RBC fatty acids>8% cardioprotective. <4% = high risk
1
Supplement ProtocolOmega-3 3–4g/day ★★★ (REDUCE-IT: 25% ↓ CV events). CoQ10 ubiquinol 200mg ★★★ (Q-SYMBIO: 43% ↓ major CV events in HF). Nattokinase 2000–4000 FU/day ★★. Hawthorn 900mg ★★. Aged garlic 600–900mg ★★. Vit K2 MK-7 180mcg ★★★ (57% ↓ coronary calcification). Magnesium 400mg ★★★.
2
CAC ScoreCoronary artery calcium (CT) is the most powerful CV event predictor. Score 0 = very low risk regardless of cholesterol. Score ≥300 = very high risk. Available in India ₹2,000–5,000. Guides statin decisions and motivates lifestyle change.

cards.title: Atrial Fibrillation — Naturopathic Management
cards._legacyHtml:
⚠️ Stroke Risk — Anticoagulation Is a Medical Decision
AF increases stroke risk 5×. CHA₂DS₂-VASc determines anticoagulation — MEDICAL decision. BNYS role: identify modifiable triggers, support rhythm maintenance, evidence-based supplementation.
Modifiable TriggerPrevalenceIntervention
OSA50–75% of AFSTOP-BANG every AF patient. CPAP reduces AF recurrence 50%.
AlcoholDose-dependentAbstinence — most effective single intervention for paroxysmal AF
HypomagnesaemiaVery common in AFIV Mg used in hospital for AF conversion. Oral Mg 400mg maintenance ★★★
Thyroid dysfunction10–15% of new AFCheck TSH in EVERY new AF. Subclinical hyperthyroid (TSH <0.5) = 3× AF risk
Inflammationhs-CRP ↑ in AFAnti-inflammatory diet, omega-3, Vit D, curcumin
1
Supplement ProtocolMagnesium glycinate 400mg ★★★. Taurine 2–3g/day ★★. CoQ10 200mg ★★. Omega-3 3g ★★★. Hawthorn 900mg ★★. Potassium-rich diet (K 4.0–5.0 mmol/L — hypokalaemia triggers AF).
🟡 Anticoagulation Safety
AF on warfarin: NEVER give St John's Wort (↓ warfarin 50%), Ginkgo, high-dose fish oil >3g. On DOACs: SJW still absolutely contraindicated. Check anticoagulant before prescribing any herb or supplement to AF patients — write this into your protocol.

cards.title: Advanced Lipid Testing & Interpretation
cards._legacyHtml:
Beyond Standard Lipid Panel
Standard lipid panel misses 50% of individuals who will have a CV event. Advanced testing provides complete picture of atherogenic particle burden.
TestWhy SuperiorOptimal Level
ApoBTotal atherogenic particles — 1 ApoB per particle. Better predictor in IR where LDL-C "discordance" is common.<0.8 g/L (primary); <0.65 (secondary prevention)
Lp(a)Independent pro-atherogenic/pro-thrombotic. Genetically determined. In 1:5 people at high levels. MISSED on standard panel.<30 mg/dL. Test once — lifelong value.
sdLDLSmall dense LDL — 3× more atherogenic. TG:HDL ratio >3.5 = Pattern B (sdLDL dominant)sdLDL <35 mg/dL
Omega-3 IndexEPA+DHA as % of RBC fatty acids — 3-month tissue status>8% cardioprotective. Achievable with 3–4g EPA+DHA/day
TG:HDL ratioFree surrogate for IR and sdLDL Pattern B<1.5 optimal. >3.5 = IR + Pattern B
🟡 South Asian Lipid Pattern
South Asians commonly have: TG:HDL ratio >3.5 even with "normal" LDL, reflecting insulin resistance and Pattern B dyslipidaemia. ApoB measurement is especially important in this population — LDL-C dramatically underestimates atherogenic burden. Request ApoB + TG:HDL ratio in every South Asian patient with metabolic risk factors, regardless of LDL-C level.

cards.title: Cardiac Rehabilitation — Naturopathic Role
cards._legacyHtml:
Cardiac Rehab — Most Underprescribed Evidence-Based Intervention
CR reduces all-cause mortality 20–25% and re-infarction 30% after MI. BNYS can deliver the naturopathic components: exercise prescription, nutrition, stress management, smoking cessation.
CR PhaseTimelineBNYS Role
Phase 1 — InpatientDays 1–5Patient education, smoking cessation advice, diet counselling
Phase 2 — Early outpatient2–6 weeksLight exercise supervision, Mediterranean diet, omega-3, CoQ10, stress reduction
Phase 3 — Supervised CR6–12 weeksGroup exercise (aerobic + resistance), dietary counselling, psychosocial support
Phase 4 — MaintenanceLifelongPrimary naturopathic provider — exercise, diet, supplements, annual CV review
1
Post-MI Supplement ProtocolOmega-3 4g ★★★, CoQ10 200–300mg ★★★, Mg glycinate 400mg, Vit K2 180mcg, Nattokinase 2000 FU BD, Vit D 3000 IU, Hawthorn 900mg, Selenium 100–200mcg.
2
Post-MI Depression30% of post-MI patients develop depression — independently doubles reinfarction risk. Screen with PHQ-9 at every visit. EPA-dominant omega-3 2g + exercise ★★★ + yoga ★★. Refer if PHQ-9 ≥10 — post-MI depression is a cardiovascular emergency.

cards.title: Varicose Veins & CVI — Naturopathic Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
Chronic Venous Insufficiency (CVI)
Affects 25% women, 15% men. Left untreated: skin changes, venous eczema, venous ulcers. Naturopathic management highly effective for mild-moderate CVI.
CEAPFeaturesManagement
C0–C1Spider veins — cosmeticNaturopathic protocol, compression, avoid prolonged standing
C2 Varicose veinsTortuous dilated veinsCompression + naturopathic. Sclerotherapy/EVLA if bothersome.
C3 OedemaAnkle swelling, worse at day endCompression ★★★, elevation, diuretic herbs, Mg
C4 Skin changesHyperpigmentation, lipodermatosclerosisVascular surgery referral + aggressive naturopathic
C5–C6 UlcerationActive/healed venous ulcerURGENT vascular referral. Manuka honey dressings ★★★
1
PhytotherapyHorse chestnut (HCSE) 300mg BD standardised to 50mg aescin ★★★ (Cochrane: comparable to compression for CVI symptoms). Grape seed OPC 150–300mg ★★★. Diosmin + hesperidin (Daflon) ★★★. Butcher's broom 150mg TID ★★.
2
Physical MeasuresCompression 20–30 mmHg Class 2 ★★★. Elevation 30 min 3×/day. Walking 30 min daily (calf pump). Hydrotherapy: contrast leg douches ★★. Avoid: prolonged standing, heat, constricting clothing.
